reason: Not automatable — Glama submission requires interactive web-based GitHub OAuth authentication with a public GitHub repository URL. my-automaton has no public GitHub repository, is not published on npm, its MCP endpoint returns HTTP 404, and no GitHub credentials are available in this environment.

glama_submission_requirements.submission_method: Web UI only — https://glama.ai/mcp/servers (Add Server button)
glama_submission_requirements.authentication: GitHub OAuth (required, no API key alternative)
glama_submission_requirements.required_input: GitHub repository URL of the MCP server
glama_submission_requirements.access_requirement: Submitter must have write/admin access to the repository
glama_submission_requirements.notes: No API-based submission endpoint was identified. All submissions flow through the client-side web interface with interactive OAuth.

my_automaton_actual_state.description: A lightweight MCP server for automation tasks
my_automaton_actual_state.endpoint_status: HTTP 404 — Not Found (service not deployed or unavailable)
my_automaton_actual_state.github_repository: None found — no public GitHub repository exists for my-automaton (GitHub API search returned 0 results)
my_automaton_actual_state.npm_package: Not found — my-automaton is not published to npm

blockers.detail: Glama exclusively uses a web UI with GitHub OAuth. There is no programmatic REST/GraphQL API to submit a server listing.
A GitHub repository URL is the sole input required by Glama. my-automaton has no public GitHub repository to submit.
No GITHUB_TOKEN or other GitHub credentials are available in the environment. Interactive GitHub OAuth cannot be completed from CLI.
The my-automaton MCP endpoint (https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp) returns HTTP 404, indicating the service is not currently deployed.
Glama auto-discovers from npm. my-automaton is not published there, so automatic discovery cannot pick it up.

automatic_discovery_possibility.note: Glama also auto-discovers servers from the official MCP Registry, npm, and GitHub Awesome Lists. If my-automaton were published to npm or listed in the official registry, it could be discovered automatically without manual submission.
recommended_prerequisites: Create a public GitHub repository for my-automaton with the MCP server source code
Publish the MCP server package to npm
Ensure the server builds successfully (with a Dockerfile)
Have the maintainer manually submit via Glama's web UI at https://glama.ai/mcp/servers using their own GitHub OAuth authentication

details.recommendation: For Glama submission to be possible, the maintainer of my-automaton should: (1) create a public GitHub repository containing the MCP server source code, (2) publish it to npm for potential automatic discovery, (3) ensure the server builds and runs with a Dockerfile, (4) manually submit the repository URL via Glama's web UI at https://glama.ai/mcp/servers using their own GitHub OAuth authentication. Until these prerequisites are met, submission to Glama is not feasible.
